tools.name: search_n_top
tools.description: Search across the nTop knowledge base to find relevant information, code examples, API references, and guides. Use this tool when you need to answer questions about nTop, find specific documentation, understand how features work, or locate implementation details. The search returns contextual conten

tools.name: query_docs_filesystem_n_top
tools.description: Run a read-only shell-like query against a virtualized, in-memory filesystem rooted at `/` that contains ONLY the nTop documentation pages and OpenAPI specs. This is NOT a shell on any real machine — nothing runs on the user's computer, the server host, or any network. The filesystem is a sandbox ba

tools.name: submit_feedback
tools.description: Report a problem with this documentation site so the docs team can fix it. Use when a documentation page is incorrect, outdated, confusing, incomplete, or has a broken example. This is for feedback about the documentation content itself — not for product support requests or feedback about this tool

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "query_docs_filesystem_n_top" references sensitive artifacts (apikey). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Run a read-only shell-like query against a virtualized, in-memory filesystem rooted at `/` that contains ONLY the nTop documentation pages and OpenAPI specs. Th…

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "query_docs_filesystem_n_top" appears informational but accepts parameters (command) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: query_docs_filesystem_n_top · Private data: query_docs_filesystem_n_top · Public sink: submit_feedback · Destructive: query_docs_filesystem_n_top.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: query_docs_filesystem_n_top · Private data: query_docs_filesystem_n_top · Public sink: submit_feedback · Destructive: query_docs_filesystem_n_top

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
